name: Purifaaya
description: Purifaaya is a ceramic water filter manufactured and distributed by SPOUTS in Uganda.

taxonomy.sub_category: Purifaaya Updated on December 7, 2023 · Created on August 24, 2017 Purifaaya Open-source Purifaaya is a ceramic water filter manufactured and distributed by SPOUTS in Uganda. Developed By SPOUTS of WATER Tested By National water quality reference laboratory (Uganda) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Purifaaya is a locally-made, ceramic water filter in Uganda that uses both physical filtration and chemical disinfection. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 2: Zero Hunger Market Suggested Retail Price $25.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Purifaaya has distributors all over Uganda. SPOUTS partners and supporters include SIMBA telecom, Tuskys Supermarket, Uganda Ministry of Water and Environment and others (page 8). Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include DHAN Biosand Filters , CeraMaji , BushProof Biosand Filter , Ideas at Work Biosand Filter , and Hydraid® BioSand Filter . Countries Uganda Manufacturing/Building Method Purifaaya is mass produced at the SPOUTS factory in Uganda. The raw materials (black clay, yellow clay, sawdust, and grog) are grinded and mixed together. The mixture is pressed into the filter form and touched up. Filters are fired in a wood-burning kiln at 900 °C. The sawdust is combusted leaving micropores, the main form of physical filtration. Filters are painted with silver nitrate for chemical disinfection. They are then packaged and distributed to various partners. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model User can purchase the product from local partners and distributors. Distributions to Date Status According to the 2016 Annual Report, roughly 10,000 units have been distributed from 2015 to 2016. Type of filter Ceramic pot filter Primary material of construction Ceramic, silver nitrate Additives Unknown Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 2 Bacteria reduction 4 (99.99%) E. coli reduction Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) >94% turbidity reduction Maintenance schedule Clean the filter about every 2 weeks Interview with representative Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 10 Safe water storage integration 10 L Design Specifications Water is filtered through small silver-impregnated pours in the ceramic filter where bacteria is too large to fit through. Product Schematics Technical Support Available from manufacturer. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Replacement Components Manufacturer recommends replacing the filter every 2-3 years to keep filtering process from slowing down. The filter will always remain effective separating out impurities. Additional replacements available from manufacture. <sup>Interview with representative</sup> Lifecycle 2 years Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters 2 L of water is filtered per hour and each filter has a life span of 2 years, or 17,520 hours. 99.9% of bacteria is eliminated. Vetted Performance Status Water collected through filter tap was tested for bacteriological, physical and chemical constituents to ensure the safety of the water and compared to the water before it went through the filter. The percentage removal rate was calculated and it’s sustainability. Purifaaya has also successfully performed in disaster relief situations like Tsunami in Sri Lanka and Flooding in Dominician Republic. More info here. Safety Regular cleaning and maintenance of ceramic water filter required in every 14 days. Complementary Technical Systems None Academic Research and References R. Available: https://spouts.org/products/ “Our Story,” SPOUTS International, 04-Nov-2021. Available: https://spouts.org/our-story/ Spouts.org. (n.d.). Sdgs.Un.Org. available: https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal6 Spouts.org. Available: https://spouts.org/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/SPOUTS_2016_Annual_Report.pdf Spouts.org. Available: https://spouts.org/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/SPOUTS-Annual-Report-2015_vF.pdf “Directorate of Water Development,” Mwe.go.ug. Available: https://www.mwe.go.ug/directorates/directorate-water-development Compliance with regulations Meets standard of safe drinking water by the Uganda Ministry of Water and Environment. Evaluation methods Laboratory testing of water cleaned by Purifaaya include parameters like conductivity, pH and turbidity measurements and testing total Coliforms and E. coli by decomposing organic matter. Other Information Awards Annual Reports: 2015 , 2016 Donations are also accepted by SPOUTS Impact of Purifaaya Spouts also offer Purifaaya XL model for schools and refugee camps Comments from the Community 1 Comment Rachel Geiger says: September 1, 2024 at 5:36 am The Purifaaya page in the solutions library needs review.
